title: CLAUDE.md
text: nemo R package tidying exploring outputs bioinformatic pipelines. uses R6 classes define Tools (parsers specific file types) Workflows (collections Tools).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tool: Base R6 class (R/Tool.R) parses specific file types outputs tidy tibbles Workflow: R6 class (R/Workflow.R) orchestrates multiple Tools, handling file discovery, tidying, writing Config: R6 class (R/Config.R) reads per-tool LinkML schema (inst/config/tools/<tool>/schema.yaml) exposes raw/tidy schemas tibbles Tools inherit base Tool class implement file-specific parsing logic

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tool single LinkML (https://github.com/linkml/linkml) schema inst/config/tools/<tool>/schema.yaml.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: prefix stripped lowercased get table name used Config API (e.g. RawTable1 → "table1").

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Slot-level in_subset tags declare tool versions field belongs . Version subsets anything isn’t raw tidy (e.g. v1.2.3, latest). slot-level version tags exist, class-level in_subset used.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Since LinkML native concept file discovery, nemo-specific metadata stored class annotations: - name — explicit logical table name (e.g. table1). Required classes. Decouples class name table name, allows multiple tidy classes share logical name (e.g. TidyTable1Coords TidyTable1Metrics name: table1, distinguished subtbl) - pattern — regex used match raw output files - ftype — file type (tsv, txt-nohead, etc.) - subtbl — tidy sub-table name (defaults "tbl1" omitted). Use distinct values one raw table produces multiple tidy tibbles

title: CLAUDE.md
text: LinkML range types map readr col spec codes internally:

title: CLAUDE.md
text: linkml_type_remap(x) — maps LinkML type → nemo internal type linkml_classes_by_subset(schema, subset) — filter classes subset tag linkml_class_name(cls, cls_name, prefix) — get logical table name; prefers annotations$name, falls back stripping prefix lowercasing linkml_strip_prefix(x, prefix) — strip class name prefix lowercase (used fallback lkml_class_name) linkml_class_versions(cls) — get version tags class linkml_slots_for_version(cls, v) — get slots belonging version

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tool1 (R/Tool1.R) Workflow1 (R/Workflow1.R) canonical examples follow creating new tools workflows. demonstrate full pattern: schema config, file discovery, raw parsing, tidy output.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: CLI built argparse via nemo_cli() (R/cli.R). entry script lives inst/cli/nemo.R. Two subcommands available: subcommands accept -w WORKFLOW (workflow name) -q (quiet). tidy subcommand also accepts --include/--exclude (comma-separated file filters) --dbname/--dbuser database output.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests generated automatically roxytest @testexamples blocks roxygen documentation. write test files manually — add @examples (example ) @testexamples (expect_* assertions) function’s roxygen block, run devtools::document() regenerate test files tests/testthat/.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R code formatted air (100-char line width, 2-space indent, configured air.toml). Pre-commit hooks (.pre-commit-config.yaml) enforce: - air-format — auto-formats R files commit - check-added-large-files — blocks files > 200 KB - file-contents-sorter — keeps .Rbuildignore sorted - check-yaml — validates YAML syntax - forbid--commit — blocks .Rhistory, .RData, .Rds, .rds files

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Bumping done via .github/workflows/bump.yaml GitHub Actions workflow, installs package, bumps version references, renders README.qmd, pushes single Bump version: OLD => NEW commit directly branch (using bot token bypass branch protection). deploy.yaml workflow triggers commit message pattern. Trigger CLI: calls gh workflow run bump.yaml --ref BRANCH --field version=VERSION hood. Can also triggered via GitHub Actions UI: Actions → Bump Version → Run workflow.
code: make bump VERSION=x.y.z BRANCH=dev # or on main: make bump VERSION=x.y.z BRANCH=main

title: CLAUDE.md
text: conda-docs workflow triggers pushes main dev, runs commit message starts Bump version:. jobs call reusable workflows tidywf/.github.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: condarise_and_tag — delegates tidywf/.github/.github/workflows/condarise--tag.yaml: 1. Builds conda package rattler-build 2. Uploads Anaconda tidywf owner; uses --channel dev label dev 3. Regenerates conda lock file (deploy/conda/env/lock/conda-linux-64.lock) linux-64 4. Commits pushes updated lock file bot commit 5. Creates git tag (vVERSION) — branches pkgdownise (depends condarise_and_tag) — delegates tidywf/.github/.github/workflows/pkgdownise.yaml: - Checks release tag, deploys via pkgdown::deploy_to_branch() — flow branches

title: CLAUDE.md
text: write() nemofy() methods support: - tsv - Tab-separated values (gzipped: .tsv.gz) - csv - Comma-separated values (gzipped: .csv.gz) - parquet - Apache Parquet format - rds - R serialized format - db - Database (requires dbconn parameter)

title: Contribute
text: Adding new tool {nemo} child package consists following steps: Data Add subsets data generated tool interest inst/extdata/<tool>. Usually top 10 rows (plus header) straightforward TSV file fine. DVC recommended tracking files version control. See vignette("devnotes") DVC notes. Configs Use inst/scripts/file_to_yaml.R script construct raw YAML config files interest. tidy YAML configs can prepared copying raw config tidying field names using snake_case. Copilot fairly good constructing raw tidy files open side side. Pasting tables describing fields tool’s official documentation can also help Copilot field description. Parsers Create parsers R/<Tool>.R. Use one tool files template.

title: Devnotes
text: Instead committing big data files git, can store files remotely (e.g. Google Drive, AWS S3, Cloudflare R2) just keep track data updates git using DVC. R package uses data files running code examples tests, rendering vignettes. simple dvc pull pull data remote storage allow processes take place.

title: Installation

title: Schema
text: common case: one raw file, one tidy tibble, latest version . classes carry in_subset: [raw, latest] / [tidy, latest] class level, applies attributes. tool1 schema included {nemo} demonstrates pattern table2. can inspect programmatically: tool’s output schema changes versions, slot-level in_subset tags declare fields belong version. class-level in_subset include raw (tidy); version membership inferred slots. Config API handles version resolution automatically: schema_guess() function (called internally parse_file()) uses exact column-count column-name matching select correct version given file automatically. tools write key-value fixed-column files without header row. Set ftype: txt-nohead list column names positional order. parser assigns names order appear attributes. corresponding tool1/table3.tsv file header, columns bound position: Sometimes single raw file parsed multiple tidy tibbles (e.g. normalisation file mixes different entity types). Use distinct subtbl annotation tidy class; share name. tidy method retrieves subtable name: raw file format irregular generic readr-based parser (e.g. samtools flagstat output), Tool subclass implements parse_* method. raw class schema still needs exist file discovery (pattern matching), attributes section single placeholder: tidy class written normally, custom parse_flagstats() method returns tibble whose columns match TidyFlagstats exactly. YAML keys contain dot (.) must quoted, otherwise YAML parses incorrectly: applies special character field name. Without quotes key silently misread. Tool’s tidy_* method calls self$.tidy_file(x, "parser") intermediate step custom R code splits reshapes result, Tidy* class must declare columns present rename step — just final output columns. example, tidy_genecvg() calls .tidy_file() (renames columns CamelCase snake_case) splits wide tibble two narrow tibbles (genes per-base coverage). schema’s TidyGenecvg class must list ~70 renamed columns (sucks know). Rule thumb: Tidy* class must match whatever colnames() tibble point get_tidy_schema() called tidy_* method.
code: subsets:   raw:     description: Raw input file schema   tidy:     description: Tidy output schema   latest:     description: Tool1 latest version  classes:   RawTable2:     description: Table2 raw file.     in_subset: [raw, latest]     annotations:       name: table2       pattern: "\\.tool1\\.table2\\.tsv$"       ftype: tsv     attributes:       SampleID:         range: string       metricA:         range: string       metricB:         range: float    TidyTable2:     description: Table2 tidy output.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: table2       subtbl: tbl1     attributes:       sample_id:         range: string         description: Sample ID       metric_a:         range: string         description: Metric A       metric_b:         range: float         description: Metric B conf <- Config$new("tool1", pkg = "nemo") conf$get_raw_schema("table2") #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   name   tbl_description  version field    type  #>   <chr>  <chr>            <chr>   <chr>    <chr> #> 1 table2 Table2 raw file. latest  SampleID c     #> 2 table2 Table2 raw file. latest  metricA  c     #> 3 table2 Table2 raw file. latest  metricB  d conf$get_tidy_schema("table2") #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   name   tbl_description     version tbl   field     type  description #>   <chr>  <chr>               <chr>   <chr> <chr>     <chr> <chr>       #> 1 table2 Table2 tidy output. latest  tbl1  sample_id c     Sample ID   #> 2 table2 Table2 tidy output. latest  tbl1  metric_a  c     Metric A    #> 3 table2 Table2 tidy output. latest  tbl1  metric_b  d     Metric B classes:   RawTable1:     description: Table1 raw file.     in_subset:       - raw            # <-- version tags omitted; slots carry them     annotations:       name: table1       pattern: "\\.tool1\\.table1\\.tsv$"       ftype: tsv     attributes:       SampleID:         range: string         in_subset: [v1.2.3, latest]   # present in both versions       Chromosome:         range: string         in_subset: [v1.2.3, latest]       Start:         range: integer         in_subset: [v1.2.3, latest]       End:         range: integer         in_subset: [v1.2.3, latest]       metricX:         range: float         in_subset: [v1.2.3, latest]       metricY:         range: float         in_subset: [latest]           # added after v1.2.3       metricZ:         range: float         in_subset: [latest]           # added after v1.2.3 conf$get_raw_schema("table1", v = "v1.2.3") # 5 fields #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   name   tbl_description  version field      type  #>   <chr>  <chr>            <chr>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 table1 Table1 raw file. v1.2.3  SampleID   c     #> 2 table1 Table1 raw file. v1.2.3  Chromosome c     #> 3 table1 Table1 raw file. v1.2.3  Start      i     #> 4 table1 Table1 raw file. v1.2.3  End        i     #> 5 table1 Table1 raw file. v1.2.3  metricX    d conf$get_raw_schema("table1", v = "latest") # 7 fields #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   name   tbl_description  version field      type  #>   <chr>  <chr>            <chr>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  SampleID   c     #> 2 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  Chromosome c     #> 3 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  Start      i     #> 4 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  End        i     #> 5 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  metricX    d     #> 6 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  metricY    d     #> 7 table1 Table1 raw file. latest  metricZ    d conf$get_raw_versions() #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   name   value  #>   <chr>  <chr>  #> 1 table1 v1.2.3 #> 2 table1 latest #> 3 table2 latest #> 4 table3 latest RawTable3:     description: Table3 raw file (no header).     in_subset: [raw, latest]     annotations:       name: table3       pattern: "\\.tool1\\.table3\\.tsv$"       ftype: txt-nohead     attributes:       SampleID:         range: string       QCStatus:         range: string       TotalReads:         range: float       MappedReads:         range: float       UnmappedReads:         range: float conf$get_raw_schema("table3") #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   name   tbl_description              version field         type  #>   <chr>  <chr>                        <chr>   <chr>         <chr> #> 1 table3 Table3 raw file (no header). latest  SampleID      c     #> 2 table3 Table3 raw file (no header). latest  QCStatus      c     #> 3 table3 Table3 raw file (no header). latest  TotalReads    d     #> 4 table3 Table3 raw file (no header). latest  MappedReads   d     #> 5 table3 Table3 raw file (no header). latest  UnmappedReads d TidySummaryTbl1:     description: Summary metrics.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: summary       # same logical name       subtbl: tbl1        # different subtable     attributes:       metric_a:         range: float         description: Metric A       metric_b:         range: float         description: Metric B    TidySummaryCovx:     description: Coverage per depth bucket.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: summary       # same logical name       subtbl: tbl2        # different subtable     attributes:       covx:         range: integer         description: Coverage depth threshold       value:         range: float         description: Fraction of bases >= covx schema_tbl1 <- self$get_tidy_schema("summary", subtbl = "tbl1") schema_tbl2 <- self$get_tidy_schema("summary", subtbl = "tbl2") RawFlagstats:     description: Counts of alignments for each FLAG type.     in_subset: [raw, latest]     annotations:       name: flagstats       pattern: "\\.flagstat$"       ftype: txt     attributes:       dummy:         range: string attributes:       "build.date":         range: string TidyGenecvg:     # All ~70 post-rename columns must be listed here so that     # get_tidy_schema() can build the correct readr column spec.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: genecvg       subtbl: tbl1     attributes:       gene:         range: string         description: Gene name       chrom:         range: string         description: Chromosome       pos_start:         range: integer         description: Start position       # ...

title: Schema
text: Sometimes single raw file parsed multiple tidy tibbles (e.g. normalisation file mixes different entity types). Use distinct subtbl annotation tidy class; share name. tidy method retrieves subtable name:
code: TidySummaryTbl1:     description: Summary metrics.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: summary       # same logical name       subtbl: tbl1        # different subtable     attributes:       metric_a:         range: float         description: Metric A       metric_b:         range: float         description: Metric B    TidySummaryCovx:     description: Coverage per depth bucket.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: summary       # same logical name       subtbl: tbl2        # different subtable     attributes:       covx:         range: integer         description: Coverage depth threshold       value:         range: float         description: Fraction of bases >= covx schema_tbl1 <- self$get_tidy_schema("summary", subtbl = "tbl1") schema_tbl2 <- self$get_tidy_schema("summary", subtbl = "tbl2")

title: Schema
text: raw file format irregular generic readr-based parser (e.g. samtools flagstat output), Tool subclass implements parse_* method. raw class schema still needs exist file discovery (pattern matching), attributes section single placeholder: tidy class written normally, custom parse_flagstats() method returns tibble whose columns match TidyFlagstats exactly.

title: Schema
text: YAML keys contain dot (.) must quoted, otherwise YAML parses incorrectly: applies special character field name. Without quotes key silently misread.
code: attributes:       "build.date":         range: string

what: 7. Intermediate tidy form (raw rename before custom split)
title: Schema
text: Tool’s tidy_* method calls self$.tidy_file(x, "parser") intermediate step custom R code splits reshapes result, Tidy* class must declare columns present rename step — just final output columns. example, tidy_genecvg() calls .tidy_file() (renames columns CamelCase snake_case) splits wide tibble two narrow tibbles (genes per-base coverage). schema’s TidyGenecvg class must list ~70 renamed columns (sucks know). Rule thumb: Tidy* class must match whatever colnames() tibble point get_tidy_schema() called tidy_* method.
code: TidyGenecvg:     # All ~70 post-rename columns must be listed here so that     # get_tidy_schema() can build the correct readr column spec.     in_subset: [tidy, latest]     annotations:       name: genecvg       subtbl: tbl1     attributes:       gene:         range: string         description: Gene name       chrom:         range: string         description: Chromosome       pos_start:         range: integer         description: Start position       # ...

title: Structure
text: intro patterns descriptions versions schemas Let’s look information raw Tool1 config, instance: can access individual fields classic R list-like manner, using $ sign. Patterns used fish relevant files directory listing. Tool1 raw file patterns. File descriptions based available open source documentation. Tool1 raw file descriptions. Versions used distinguish changes schema individual tool versions. example, Tool1 v1.2.3 metrics Y Z added table1, reflected available schemas. now using latest default version based recent schema tests, discrepancies see labelled accordingly version tool generated file different schema. Tool1 raw file versions. raw schemas specify column name type (e.g. character (c), integer (), float/double (d)) input file:

title: Tidy Bioinformatic Pipeline Outputs
text: {nemo} R package contains building blocks parsing, tidying, writing bioinformatic pipeline results consistent structure. short, traverses directory containing results one runs certain bioinformatic tools, parses files recognises, tidies (includes data reshaping, normalisation, column name cleanup etc.), writes output format choice e.g. Apache Parquet, PostgreSQL, TSV, RDS. specific tools can handle controlled configuration files written YAML part ‘child’ {nemo} packages, like {tidywigits} {dracarys}. configuration files (inst/config respective packages) specify schemas, types, patterns field descriptions raw input files tidy output tbls.

title: Write data — nemo_write
code: d <- tibble::tibble(name = "foo", data = 123) fpfix <- file.path(tempdir(), "data_test1") format <- "csv" nemo_write(d = d, fpfix = fpfix, format = format) (res <- readr::read_csv(glue::glue("{fpfix}.csv.gz"), show_col_types = FALSE)) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   name   data #>   <chr> <dbl> #> 1 foo     123 if (FALSE) { # RPostgres::postgresHasDefault() # for database writing con <- DBI::dbConnect(RPostgres::Postgres()) tbl_nm <- "awesome_tbl" nemo_write(d = d, fpfix = basename(fpfix), format = "db", dbconn = con, dbtab = tbl_nm) DBI::dbListTables(con) DBI::dbReadTable(con, tbl_nm) DBI::dbDisconnect(con) }

title: Guess Schema — schema_guess
code: dir1 <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo") fpath1 <- file.path(dir1, "latest", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv") fpath2 <- file.path(dir1, "v1.2.3", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv") pname <- "table1" cnames1 <- file_hdr(fpath1) cnames2 <- file_hdr(fpath2) conf <- Config$new("tool1", pkg = "nemo") schemas_all <- conf$get_raw_schemas_all() (s1 <- schema_guess(pname, cnames1, schemas_all)) #> $schema #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>   field      type  #>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 SampleID   c     #> 2 Chromosome c     #> 3 Start      i     #> 4 End        i     #> 5 metricX    d     #> 6 metricY    d     #> 7 metricZ    d     #>  #> $version #> [1] "latest" #>  (s2 <- schema_guess(pname, cnames2, schemas_all)) #> $schema #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   field      type  #>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 SampleID   c     #> 2 Chromosome c     #> 3 Start      i     #> 4 End        i     #> 5 metricX    d     #>  #> $version #> [1] "v1.2.3" #>
